world.title: Does Trump's wild F-bomb online rant show that cracks are appearing?
world.description: Donald Trump's extraordinary post on Truth Social yesterday has led to questions over whether he has a strategy for the war in Iran.

world.title: How unsupervised screen time harms vulnerable preschoolers
world.description: Early problems with language can have a lasting negative impact on social and emotional development. Building on this foundation, a new groundbreaking study from Florida Atlantic University and Aarhus University in Denmark tests the hypothesis that unsupervised, solitary screen time during early childhood increases the likelihood that language difficulties will lead to socioemotional difficulties.

world.title: Artemis II astronauts race to set a new distance record from Earth and behold the moon's far side
world.description: HOUSTON — With the moon looming ever larger, the Artemis II astronauts raced to set a new distance record Monday from Earth on a lunar fly-around promising magnificent views of the far side never seen before by eye.

world.title: Ethiopia Arrests Major Human Trafficking Network - Solda Safety Network Praises Breakthrough
Iran rejects ceasefire proposal, wants permanent end to war as deadline nears on Trump ultimatum
world.description: The US president has threatened to rain "hell" on Tehran if it did not make a deal that would allow traffic to start moving again through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

world.title: The U.S. Navy Has No Way to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
world.description: Five weeks into operation Epic Fury, the Strait of Hormuz remains closed — 20% of the world's oil, helium for semiconductor manufacturing, and fertilizer for global agriculture are all blocked. Nations are already paying Iran tolls in Chinese yuan to pass through, undermining the dollar's reserve status. Military experts told Defense News that reopening Hormuz faces five critical problems: Iranian geography and fortified coastline, sophisticated mines, insurance market confidence, zero allied support, and inevitable escalation. The easiest part is starting the operation — the hardest is convincing global markets the strait is safe. If Trump escalates, the regime change may happen in Washington this November, not Tehran.

world.title: Global waste crisis is accelerating, and we’ve already hit 2030 levels
world.description: The world is producing more waste than expected - and many regions can’t keep up. A new report reveals the growing global crisis.

world.title: Israel's death penalty law draws inspiration from US policy, Itamar Ben-Gvir says
world.description: Per the bill, West Bank terrorists would receive the automatic death penalty barring specific appeals, while terrorists in other areas would be given either capital punishment or life imprisonment.

science.title: A secret odorant code patches a problematic relationship between pollinators and flowers
science.description: A plant uses a rare scent to guide its pollinator to male flowers first and to female flowers later, finds a study led by Kobe University. The work, appearing in Current Biology, uncovers a precise chemical system that not only keeps this partnership specific but also helps ensure it remains beneficial to both parties.

ai.title: Samsung is discontinuing its texting app, tells impacted users to switch to Google Messages
ai.description: Samsung is phasing out its own messaging app. Users of Samsung phones in the US will need to switch to Google Messages by July. This change aims to provide a consistent Android experience. Google Messages offers advanced features like AI-powered suggestions and better photo sharing. Older Android versions will not be affected. Samsung has not confirmed if this applies globally.
